agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a fee, time-window commitment, and refund policy. Raw stakeholder notes from a product discovery workshop reveal fundamental conflicts about the nature of the delivery promise.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — covers order eligibility, driver assignment, time-window enforcement, fee structure, refund policy, and real-time tracking.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a 2-hour delivery window and additional fee, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"A $12.99 surcharge for express delivery, separate from the base delivery fee."},{"term":"Express Window","definition":"The 2-hour period from order placement within which delivery should be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute window within which a driver must pick up an express order from the warehouse after placement."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery fee charged regardless of delivery tier; not refundable even on express window miss."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"A refund of the express fee issued when the express window is missed — Legal classifies this as a goodwill gesture, not a contractual obligation."},{"term":"Express-Eligible Driver","definition":"A driver with a rating of 4 stars or above, qualified to fulfill express orders."},{"term":"Driver Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute window to assign a replacement driver when the original driver cancels mid-delivery; failure triggers automatic refund."},{"term":"Wrong Address Exception","definition":"A refund exclusion: no refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — express timing is impossible beyond this radius. Violating it guarantees a broken promise.","testable_assertion":"Given a customer address >10km from all warehouses, the express delivery option must not be selectable."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Express delivery charges an additional fee of $12.99 on top of the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Price is a business policy that can be changed by Finance.","testable_assertion":"When a customer selects express delivery, the order total includes a $12.99 express fee line item."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operations states the 2-hour window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes — this is an operational constraint that protects the delivery promise.","testable_assertion":"If no driver picks up the order within 15 minutes, the system must escalate (alert ops, attempt reassignment, or convert to standard)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or above may be assigned to express orders.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Rating threshold is a quality policy that Operations can adjust.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, they must never be assigned to an express order."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When the express window is missed, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy decision.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery completed after the 2-hour window, the system issues a refund for exactly the express fee amount, not the base fee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Refunds for missed express windows are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Processing timeline is an operational/finance policy.","testable_assertion":"Given a missed-window refund is triggered, it must appear in the refund queue with a processing deadline of 3 business days from trigger."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the express window is missed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Fault-based exclusion — the system must never refund when the delay is attributable to the customer, as this would create a moral hazard.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery marked as 'wrong address' by the driver, no automatic refund is issued regardless of delivery time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund of the express fee is issued.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-minute window and auto-refund are customer success policies.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver cancellation at time T, if no replacement is assigned by T+10min, the system automatically issues an express fee refund."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Customers must be able to track their express driver's location in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an active express delivery, the customer can see the driver's current GPS location updated at least every 30 seconds."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer's delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse","when":"The customer views delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is available and displays the $12.99 fee and 2-hour window"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer's delivery address is more than 10km from all warehouses","when":"The customer views delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is hidden or greyed out with explanation"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"No driver picks up the order within 15 minutes","then":"The system escalates: attempts reassignment to another express-eligible driver and alerts operations"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express delivery is completed after the 2-hour window","when":"The system evaluates the delivery outcome","then":"An express fee refund is automatically queued, processing within 3 business days"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express delivery missed its window but the driver flagged a wrong customer address","when":"The system evaluates the delivery outcome","then":"No refund is issued and the customer is notified of the reason"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A driver cancels an active express delivery","when":"10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned","then":"An automatic express fee refund is issued to the customer"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A driver cancels an active express delivery","when":"A replacement express-eligible driver is assigned within 10 minutes","then":"The delivery continues and the express window clock keeps running from original order time"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An active express delivery is in progress","when":"The customer opens the tracking view","then":"The driver's real-time GPS location is displayed, updating at a reasonable frequency"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"15-minute pickup SLA expires and no express-eligible driver is available","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — no stakeholder defined what happens. Options: convert to standard delivery with fee refund, keep trying, or cancel. Needs decision."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at minute 115 of a 120-minute window — replacement window (10 min) extends past the express window","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — does the 2-hour window miss take precedence, or does the 10-minute replacement window get its own clock? Could result in double-refund logic."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address mid-delivery to one >10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — should express guarantee be voided? Should this count as 'wrong address'?"},{"scenario":"Multiple driver cancellations on the same order","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — does each cancellation restart the 10-minute replacement clock? Is there a max retry count before auto-cancel?"},{"scenario":"Warehouse is out of stock after express order placement","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — no stakeholder addressed inventory availability checks or what happens if items are unavailable."},{"scenario":"Express order placed 1 minute before warehouse closing time","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — no mention of warehouse operating hours or cut-off times for express eligibility."},{"scenario":"Wrong address flag is disputed by the customer","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — who arbitrates? Is there an appeals process? What evidence standard applies?"},{"scenario":"Driver rating drops below 4.0 mid-delivery","expected_behavior":"Delivery should continue — the eligibility check applies at assignment time, not during transit."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Express fee of $12.99 creates a new revenue stream. However, refund exposure is significant if the 2-hour window is frequently missed. Auto-refund on driver cancellation + replacement failure adds uncontrolled refund liability. The 'wrong address' exclusion mitigates some fraud risk but needs a dispute process.","operational":"15-minute pickup SLA puts pressure on driver availability and dispatch systems. 4-star driver requirement shrinks the eligible driver pool. Real-time tracking requires GPS infrastructure. The 10-minute replacement window demands sophisticated re-dispatch logic. Operations needs monitoring dashboards for express SLA compliance.","user":"Prominent display of '2-hour guarantee' sets high expectations. Real-time tracking increases transparency but also amplifies frustration if the driver appears to be off-route. The refund policy creates goodwill but the legal positioning as 'best effort' may undermine trust if communicated poorly.","risk":"CRITICAL: The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction creates legal and reputational risk. If marketing says 'guaranteed' but terms say 'best effort,' the company is exposed to false advertising claims and customer backlash. The wrong-address dispute process is undefined, creating CS escalation risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"New premium option with time commitment. Expectation management is critical — the guarantee/best-effort conflict directly affects trust."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"New express tier with rating requirement creates a two-tier driver system. Express assignments likely pay more but have tighter SLAs. Drivers below 4 stars are excluded from this revenue."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must build or adapt dispatch to enforce 15-min pickup, 10-min replacement, 4-star filtering, and real-time tracking. Significant system changes."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"New fee revenue stream offset by refund liability. Need refund rate forecasting and 3-business-day processing pipeline."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Must resolve guarantee language before launch. Refund terms need contractual clarity. Wrong-address exclusion needs evidentiary standards."},{"group":"Customer Success","impact":"Will handle disputes around wrong-address refund denials, guarantee vs. best-effort complaints, and replacement driver delays."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, unresolved contradiction between Product/Customer Success (who call it a 'guarantee') and Legal (who insist it is 'best effort'). This is not a terminology quibble — it fundamentally changes the product promise, the refund policy's nature, and how the feature is presented to customers. Additionally, multiple operational edge cases have no defined behavior. Confidence is low because the core product promise is undefined.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99)","10km warehouse proximity eligibility rule","4-star driver rating requirement","Express fee-only refund scope (not base fee)","Wrong-address refund exclusion","3 business day refund processing window"],"low_confidence_areas":["What happens when 15-minute pickup SLA expires with no driver available","Whether the 2-hour clock resets or continues on driver replacement","Dispute resolution process for wrong-address flags","Warehouse hours and inventory checks for express eligibility","How to present the time commitment to customers given legal constraints"],"assumptions":["The 10km distance is measured as straight-line (geodesic), not driving distance","The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup","Driver rating is a rolling average, not per-delivery","Real-time tracking applies only to the delivery phase, not the pickup phase"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Product Manager and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery window as a 'guarantee' — PM says 'guaranteed within 2 hours,' CS says 'the 2-hour guarantee should be shown prominently.' Legal explicitly states: 'We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing — this is a best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee.' These positions are mutually exclusive. The product cannot simultaneously promise a guarantee and disclaim it as best effort. This affects: UI copy, refund policy enforcement (obligation vs. goodwill), terms of service language, and marketing materials.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Customer Success","Legal","Customers","Marketing"],"options":["A: Adopt Legal's position — present as 'best effort' with goodwill refund. Requires PM and CS to accept weaker language.","B: Adopt PM's position — present as a guarantee with contractual refund obligation. Requires Legal to accept liability exposure.","C: Hybrid — use 'Express Promise' branding (not 'guarantee') with automatic refund as a policy, avoiding contractual guarantee language while still setting strong expectations."],"recommendation":"Option C — a branded promise ('Express Promise' or 'Express Commitment') avoids the legal term 'guarantee' while still communicating reliability to customers. The automatic refund becomes a policy, not a contractual right. This satisfies Legal's constraint while preserving PM and CS's intent. However, this MUST be validated by Legal before implementation."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success states that failure to assign a replacement driver within 10 minutes triggers an automatic refund. Legal states the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' If the system automatically issues refunds on a 10-minute timer, this creates a de facto contractual obligation through system behavior, contradicting Legal's position that refunds are discretionary.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Legal","Finance"],"options":["A: Make all refunds manual/discretionary — CS reviews each case. Aligns with Legal but degrades customer experience.","B: Keep auto-refund but have Legal accept that systematic auto-refund effectively creates a binding practice. Update terms accordingly.","C: Auto-refund as default but allow ops override for edge cases (e.g., force majeure). Preserve automation while maintaining some discretion."],"recommendation":"Option C — auto-refund with override capability. This gives customers the reliability CS wants while preserving the discretionary framing Legal requires. Terms should state refunds are 'typically' issued, not 'guaranteed.'"}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a fee, time-window commitment, and refund policy. Raw stakeholder notes from a product discovery workshop reveal fundamental conflicts about the nature of the delivery promise.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — covers order eligibility, driver assignment, time-window enforcement, fee structure, refund policy, and real-time tracking.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definit

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these product discovery workshop notes for the "Express Delivery" product. I need to identify the primary blocker type and flag contradictions.

Key contradiction: PM says "guaranteed within 2 hours" and Customer Success says "2-hour guarantee should be shown prominently" — but Legal explicitly says "We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing" and it's "best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee." This is a direct contradiction between stakeholders.

There are also other tensions but the guarantee vs. best-effort is the clearest contradiction.

Primary blocker: `contradiction`


Now I'm documenting the full analysis with the stakeholder positions and the specific language conflicts that need resolution.
